title: Shattered Reflections in the Conservatory
openingIncident: At 8:45 pm, Eleanor Whitcombe, renowned art dealer, was found dead in the dining room table set with a glass, a bottle, a plate and a knife. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Graham Hargreaves
suspects.statement: I left at 8:30 pm. I never touched the knife and did not go near the glass.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:30 pm and did not return.

suspects.name: Clara Beaumont
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, Victor Lyle helped me set the bottle down on the side table. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Graham Hargreaves and Eleanor Whitcombe were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Victor Lyle and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Victor Lyle
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, I helped Clara Beaumont set the bottle down on the side table. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:45 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Clara Beaumont and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

clues.title: The Edge of Knife
clues.detail: A fresh line of blood runs along one edge of the knife, and its point has a new bend. Eleanor Whitcombe has a narrow wound consistent with that edge.

clues.title: Ring beneath Bottle
clues.detail: The fresh circular ring beneath the bottle is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack whose edge remains unbroken. Clara Beaumont and Victor Lyle both state that they set the bottle down on the side table at 8:35 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Knife
clues.detail: Fine copper-coloured silk threads are caught around the handle of the knife. Graham Hargreaves's copper silk pocket square has a fresh narrow tear, exposing the same alternating copper and navy stripe.

clues.title: Fragment at Glass
clues.detail: A curved black lacquer chip edged in gold is lodged inside the handle joint of the glass on top of a narrow blood droplet from the attack. The black-and-gold fountain-pen cap worn by Graham Hargreaves has a fresh curved gap in the lacquer; the exposed edges visibly fit the fragment.

clues.title: Crack in Plate
clues.detail: A crack runs across the plate. A continuous yellowed glue line is visible deep inside the break.

solution.motive: Graham Hargreaves killed Eleanor Whitcombe because the imminent exposure of his embezzlement scheme that would ruin his reputation and leave him bankrupt.
solution.method: Eleanor Whitcombe was fatally stabbed with the knife.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:35 pm and before discovery at 8:45 pm
solution.opportunity: Graham Hargreaves claimed to have left at 8:30 pm. The timeline evidence places the attack after Clara Beaumont and Victor Lyle left together at 8:35 pm; the fine copper-coloured silk threads on the knife and the black and gold fragment recovered from the glass place Graham Hargreaves inside afterwards. Graham Hargreaves therefore had the only evidenced opportunity to be alone with Eleanor Whitcombe and reach the knife during the murder window.
